Proposition 1 / prop:sharp-lp:novelty, Assumption response-types and Lemma polytope:correctness, Proposition 2 / prop:endpoint-extension:novelty, Proposition 3 / prop:point-collapse:correctness, Proposition 4 / prop:sign-boundary:mixed, Sanity checks:correctness

structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections are present, the anchor is correctly treated as PartialID, assumptions are labelled, and the formalization checklist lists the main finite objects, probabilistic assumptions, and convex-analytic identities needed downstream. The locked-parameter content matches the partial-identification tuple, although it is reformatted rather than copied verbatim.

novelty.notes: The actual derivation is below the orchestrator floor novelty_target=field. The main proved content is generic finite-LP sharpness, a generic support-face endpoint-extension criterion, a known surrogate-index no-drift corner, and a sign test that is algebraically equivalent to θ_L>0 or θ_U<0 after defining residuals from the sharp endpoints. This is at most incremental/subfield, not field-level.

correctness.notes: The point-collapse theorem uses an unstated and false implication: c(U_J(d),U_H(d))≤0 with c merely nonnegative does not imply U_H(d)=U_J(d). The finite-polytope proof also relies on response types being finite while outcomes are described as [0,1]-valued unless finiteness is imposed by assumption rather than derived. Several sharpness statements are made true by definition or by assuming the exact projection already characterizes the endpoint problem.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition 1 / prop:sharp-lp
perItemFindings.one_line: The theorem is the standard fact that a linear functional over a nonempty compact convex polytope has an attained interval image; it is not a field-level contribution.

perItemFindings.label: Assumption response-types and Lemma polytope
perItemFindings.one_line: Finite-dimensional polytope sharpness is effectively assumed by declaring T, M, and L to be exactly the generated admissible objects, so the claimed response-type construction is not independently proved.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition 2 / prop:endpoint-extension
perItemFindings.one_line: The endpoint-extension criterion is generic support-function monotonicity for nested feasible sets, not a new panel-tightening theorem for observed increments or drift.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition 3 / prop:point-collapse
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof incorrectly infers no drift from ρ=0 even though the stated cost c is only nonnegative and may have zero-cost off-diagonal transitions.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition 4 / prop:sign-boundary
perItemFindings.one_line: The sign criterion is tautological after defining r_- and r_+ from θ_L and θ_U, and the binary transport formulas are conditional on assuming Γ_d is already the exact endpoint projection.

perItemFindings.label: Sanity checks
perItemFindings.one_line: The Manski fallback and bridge-response checks are plausible, but the zero-width surrogate-index check inherits the missing zero-cost-implies-equality assumption from prop:point-collapse.

review.verbatim_critique:
The note is structurally usable as a PartialID Stage 0 artifact: it has the required sections, labelled assumptions, a cluster-aware §7 bounding functional, a distinct causal target, main theorem statements, proofs, sanity checks, minimality checks, and an actionable §14 checklist. The locked-parameter block at lines 156-177 matches the supplied partial-identification tuple in substance, though it is not a verbatim block from the proposal.

The main failure is novelty relative to the enforced field-tier floor. Proposition prop:sharp-lp, lines 265-273 and proof lines 366-378, is only the standard finite partial-identification LP sharpness argument already attributed to Manski and Balke-Pearl. Proposition prop:endpoint-extension, lines 275-285 and proof lines 380-400, is the generic fact that optimizing a linear functional over a smaller feasible set weakly tightens endpoints, plus the standard support-face intersection criterion for equality. It does not prove the proposed field-level kernel that observed noisy panel increments characterize strict tightening; indeed lines 285 and 477-478 explicitly demote observed-increment relevance to a diagnostic. Proposition prop:point-collapse is a known surrogate-index corner. Proposition prop:sign-boundary, lines 300-330 and proof lines 420-447, defines r_-=μ_SI-θ_L and r_+=θ_U-μ_SI, so the displayed sign condition is just θ_L>0 or θ_U<0 in disguise. The transport display is also conditional on Γ_d(P;ρ) being the exact projection that characterizes the full feasible endpoint problem, so the hard reduction is assumed rather than proved. Taken together, the derivation delivers at most an incremental/subfield finite-LP template, below novelty_target=field. Lifting this to field would require a genuinely new theorem: for example, a proved strict-tightening criterion in the minimal binary noisy-panel model, a nontrivial closed-form sharp transport bound, or an explicit refutation with a sharp counterexample that resolves the Stage -1 conjecture. The current artifact does not contain such a result.

There are also correctness problems. In prop:point-collapse, proof line 403 says c(U_J(d),U_H(d))≤0 permits only no-drift transitions. Assumption ass:drift at lines 200-205 states only that c is nonnegative. A nonnegative cost can have c(u,v)=0 for u≠v, so ρ=0 need not imply U_H(d)=U_J(d). The theorem needs an additional metric/separation/support assumption such as c(u,v)=0 iff u=v, or it is false. The same issue invalidates the zero-width sanity check at lines 457-462. The polytope proof also leans heavily on ass:response-types: lines 129-138 and 208-210 make T finite and M exact by convention, while outcomes are elsewhere [0,1]-valued. If Y_H(d) is genuinely continuous, T is not finite unless outcomes are discretized or replaced by bounded conditional-mean variables; if T is simply assumed finite, the LP theorem becomes a restatement of that modelling convention rather than a derived sharp response-type construction. Finally, bridge matching at lines 119-127 and 355 is asserted to be linear but the note does not spell out the latent bridge variables or equations sufficiently to verify that the observed bridge law P^B is exactly matched rather than only summarized by g and m.

Because the flagship theorem-level content collapses to generic LP/support-function machinery and one main correctness claim is false under the stated assumptions, this is not a viable accept under the field-tier floor. The repair is not a local exposition patch; it requires replacing the generic endpoint-face and residual-algebra statements with a substantive field-level derivation under the locked PartialID parameters.

reason: Case 2b (Bucket A, caveat-class cost separation) + Case 6 partial (novelty floor missed because the derivation collapsed to generic finite-LP/support-function content rather than proving the promised observed-increment strict-tightening kernel). The conjecture is salvageable — reviewer explicitly lists viable field-tier deliverables (strict-tightening criterion in the minimal binary noisy-panel model, closed-form sharp transport bound, or sharp counterexample); the failure is at the derivation layer, not the conjecture. Per-boundary heuristic suggested stage_0 over user; overriding the alternative stage_neg1 because the reviewer did not flag the conjecture as counterexample-grade.
proposed_action: Re-derive at .tex layer with the SAME conjecture. (1) Integrate the new labelled assumption A-cost-separation verbatim under §7 (place adjacent to ass:drift) and use it in the prop:point-collapse proof at lines 401-410 to legitimately conclude U_H(d)=U_J(d) from ρ=0; propagate the fix into the zero-width sanity check at lines 457-462. (2) Replace the response-types convention at lines 129-138 and 208-210 with a derived finite-response-type construction: either restrict Y_H(d) to a discretized / bounded-conditional-mean target so T is genuinely finite, or invoke a separately justified compactification argument so the polytope claim is proved rather than assumed. (3) Re-author prop:endpoint-extension (currently generic support-function monotonicity at lines 275-285, 380-400) to deliver the promised field-tier kernel — a proved strict-tightening criterion on observed noisy panel increments in the locked binary surrogate-index model, NOT a diagnostic. (4) Re-author prop:sign-boundary (lines 300-330, 420-447) so r_- and r_+ are constructed from primitive observables, not defined as μ_SI−θ_L and θ_U−μ_SI which makes the sign condition tautological. Do not introduce any OTHER unstated premise beyond A-cost-separation.
cite: prop:point-collapse proof line 403; ass:drift lines 200-205; prop:sign-boundary lines 300-330; ass:response-types lines 129-138

proposed_assumption.label: A-cost-separation
proposed_assumption.statement: The drift cost c: U × U → ℝ_{≥0} (ass:drift) is positive off the diagonal: c(u,v) = 0 if and only if u = v, for all u,v ∈ U.
proposed_assumption.source: Standard nondegeneracy condition for a transport / drift cost function. The reviewer notes that ass:drift currently only asserts c ≥ 0, which permits zero-cost off-diagonal transitions and breaks the ρ = 0 ⇒ no-drift step in prop:point-collapse. Making c separate points is the minimal generic fix; it is not the kernel claim of the proposal (the kernel is the observed-increment strict-tightening characterization).

assumption_classifications.label: A-cost-separation
assumption_classifications.one_line: Standard nondegeneracy for the drift cost c (positive definite off-diagonal); without it the term 'cost' is degenerate. The proposal's flagship strict-tightening / surrogate-index claims are unchanged outside the measure-zero degenerate-cost boundary.
